style_summary: A hand-drawn, line-art illustration style with cross-hatching for shading, used for clear, diagrammatic explanations. The visual language is primarily monochromatic but can feature muted, watercolor-like color sections to organize information, with a focus on icons and flow. CRITICAL: keep compositions minimal, uncluttered, and highly legible — use generous whitespace, one icon per concept, bold readable labels, and never duplicate section titles or labels. When in doubt, remove elements rather than add them.
simplification_rules: Labels must be large, bold, and read clearly on a phone screen
Never duplicate section titles or labels anywhere in the image
If in doubt, remove the element
roadmap_path_rules: Primary path must be a continuous S-shaped or serpentine curve that fills the full width AND height of the canvas, using the corners edge to edge
Stagger waypoint labels on alternating sides of the path (above / below, or left / right) to create visual rhythm and avoid crowding
Waypoint labels must be significantly larger than tier titles if tier titles are also present — the viewer should read the skill names first
Never render hex color codes, RGB values, or any style metadata as visible text in the image
